statement.p: In a forest, the number of deer can be modeled by the function f(t)={\frac{45t+400}{0.1t+4}}\text{,} where t stands for the number of years from now. Answer the question with technology.
As time goes on, the population levels off at approximately deer living in the forest.

solution.p:
With technology, we can find y-values of a few points when x-values are very large. For example:
(100,{350})
(1000,{436.538})
(10000,{448.606})
(100000,{449.86})
We can see the number of deer living in the forest is approaching 450\text{.}
